title: Show partially legible influence hints
overview: Add a hint registry that maps VisualParams keys to human-readable influence categories, then render those hints inside the existing info overlay. The registry serves as the single source of truth; the overlay reads from it so hints stay aligned with implemented mappings.

steps.title: Create hint registry module
steps.description: Export a typed array of hint entries, each linking one or more VisualParams keys to a category label and a short poetic description. Categories: 'location' (paletteHue, paletteSaturation), 'time' (cadence), 'device' (density), 'preference' (motionAmplitude), 'motion' (pointerDisturbance), 'sound' (bassEnergy, trebleEnergy). Export a HintEntry interface with { category: string; description: string; paramKeys: (keyof VisualParams)[] }. Export the array as INFLUENCE_HINTS. Add a compile-time completeness check that ensures every key of VisualParams appears in at least one entry.

entries.description: Where you are tints the palette
Time of day sets the rhythm
Your motion preference tempers the energy
Sound drives the motion and shimmer

details.completeness_check: Use a type-level assertion: collect all paramKeys into a union and assert it extends keyof VisualParams and vice versa. This ensures adding a new VisualParams field without a hint entry causes a compile error.

acceptance_criteria_addressed: Hints align with actual implemented mappings
Hints can be updated from a single source of truth

steps.title: Write unit tests for hint registry
steps.description: Test the registry data integrity before wiring it into UI.

tests.description: Every entry has a non-empty category string
Every entry has a non-empty description string
Every entry has at least one paramKey
Every key of VisualParams is covered by at least one entry's paramKeys
No paramKey appears in more than one entry (no duplicates across entries)
No description contains raw identifiers (IP, user-agent, exact location, country code, browser name)
Each description is under 60 characters (keeps overlay concise)

steps.title: Render hint list inside info overlay
steps.description: Import INFLUENCE_HINTS from hintRegistry. After the existing three paragraphs, append a styled <ul> with class 'eavi-hints'. Each <li> shows the category as a label and the description as body text. The list is built by iterating INFLUENCE_HINTS, so adding/removing a registry entry automatically updates the overlay. No raw identifiers appear in the rendered DOM.

details.privacy_constraint: Descriptions are static strings from the registry; no runtime signal values are interpolated into the DOM.

acceptance_criteria_addressed: Overlay names categories of influence such as time, device, location, and motion
Overlay does not reveal raw identifier values

steps.title: Add CSS for hint list
steps.description: Add styles for .eavi-hints (list-style none, padding 0, margin-top), .eavi-hints li (subtle bottom border, padding), and .eavi-hint-category (uppercase, letter-spacing, reduced opacity). Keep the overall overlay copy under the 500-char test limit by using concise descriptions. The hint list should feel like a quiet legend, not a data dashboard.

details.style_notes: Match existing overlay text color and font. Use opacity ~0.6 for category labels. No bold or bright accent colors — keep it understated.

steps.title: Write UI integration tests for hint rendering
steps.description: Add a new describe block 'US-023: Influence hints' with tests verifying the hints render correctly inside the overlay.

tests.description: Hint list contains one <li> per INFLUENCE_HINTS entry
No <li> text contains raw identifiers (IP address pattern, user-agent fragments, country codes)
Hint list renders at least entries for 'time', 'device', 'location', and 'motion'
Total overlay panel text (excluding close button) remains under 500 characters
Hint list renders correctly after toggling overlay open, closed, then open again

steps.title: Validate build and full test suite
steps.description: Run npm run build and npx vitest run. Confirm zero console errors, no forbidden storage API usage, all existing tests still pass, and the new tests pass. Manually verify the overlay renders the hint list when opened in the dev server.
checks: npx vitest run exits 0 with all tests passing
Overlay 500-char limit test still passes with hints added
Toggling overlay open/closed/open renders hints correctly each time

risks.risk: Adding hint text may push overlay copy past the 500-character limit enforced by existing test T-022
risks.mitigation: Keep each hint description under 45 chars. If still over limit, shorten existing p3 copy since the hint list now conveys the same message more specifically. Alternatively, exclude hint list text from the 500-char count by scoping the test to .eavi-info-panel > p elements only.

risks.risk: Compile-time completeness check may be unfamiliar pattern
risks.mitigation: Use a simple satisfies-based assertion or a helper type that produces never if any keys are missing. Document the pattern with a one-line comment.

reviewer_feedback_addressed.suggestion: Consider adding a 'preference' category for reduced-motion
reviewer_feedback_addressed.response: Added as a dedicated 'preference' category covering motionAmplitude, separate from device.

reviewer_feedback_addressed.suggestion: Confirm VisualParams has exactly 8 fields before relying on paramKeys validation
reviewer_feedback_addressed.response: Verified: VisualParams has exactly 8 fields (paletteHue, paletteSaturation, cadence, density, motionAmplitude, pointerDisturbance, bassEnergy, trebleEnergy). The compile-time completeness check will catch any future additions.

reviewer_feedback_addressed.suggestion: Step 6 should verify hint list renders correctly when overlay is toggled open/closed multiple times
reviewer_feedback_addressed.response: Added T-023-17 to test re-render after toggle cycle.

definition_of_done: No console errors on normal load
Scene works with and without successful autoplay
Mute toggle and info overlay still work
Overlay names categories: time, device, location, motion, preference, sound
No raw identifier values appear in overlay DOM
Hints are sourced from INFLUENCE_HINTS registry (single source of truth)
All paramKeys in registry correspond to actual VisualParams fields
